workers.name: foerdermittel-check-api (legacy/shadow)
workers.note: Pure JavaScript, 4691 LOC index.js + 523 LOC matching.js. Shares D1 DBs with main worker (bafa_antraege, foerderprogramme, foerdermittel-checks) → same data, different auth.

nextApiRoutes.summary: POST sets fund24-token HttpOnly cookie from body.token; DELETE clears it
nextApiRoutes.validation: length >= 10 only — no JWT signature check before storing

endpoints.summary: Email+password login; auto-migrates SHA-256→PBKDF2; returns JWT + refresh
Rotate refresh token, issue new access JWT
Verify email via UUID token (legacy path)
Verify 6-digit email code, mark verified, issue JWT + refresh
Resend 6-digit verification code (silent on unknown email)
Generate reset token + send email (silent on unknown email)
Reset password via UUID token, revokes refresh tokens
Revoke all refresh tokens for user
Generate 5-phase BAFA report via Workers AI; reserves 1 kontingent
Render HTML preview of a report (owner only)
HMAC-signed unlock + create download token; emails URL
Validate download token, return HTML attachment
Paginated list of own reports, enriched from BAFA_DB
Whitelisted update of report (zfbf + bafa fields)
Atomically redeem gutschein with race-safe insert+update
Create order (einzel/starter/standard/pro) with optional promo; auto-completes for 0EUR/invoice
Create Stripe checkout session for a report
Stripe webhook (signature-verified, KV-idempotent); handles checkout.session.completed + charge.refunded
Create PayPal order for a report
Capture PayPal order, validate amount, issue download token
Polls payment status by sessionId/paymentId; for PayPal performs fallback capture
Batch URL HEAD-check of aktiv programs; marks dead as abgelaufen
DSGVO Art. 17 anonymization; keeps payments per HGB §257
Distinct filter values for the catalog
List own matches enriched with program data
General AI chat with optional case context
Create case + AI-generated or fallback funnel; copies steps
List own cases enriched with program + step counts
Update step status; auto-advance phase when all required done
Multipart upload (10 MB) to R2 + DB metadata
Public list of available berater (paginated, filterable)
Public berater detail with expertise + dienstleistungen
Step 1: Submit company form, optionally scrape URL, return session+greeting
Step 2: AI chat for the check session
Step 3: Document upload to R2 + AI extraction
Step 4: Run full matching, save matches, AI summary
Step 5: Get AI-generated action plan for top matches
List own berater's bafa_beratungen with unternehmen-name join
Update phase/protokoll/foerderhoehe; emails owner on phase change

usedBy: components/shared/NotificationsBell.tsx (mounted in Navbar → every protected page)

endpoints.summary: Aggregated user dashboard (cases by status, dokumente count, reports count)
Accept/reject anfrage; auto-creates bafa_beratungen on accept
Send anfrage to a berater (any auth role)
Multipart upload (25 MB) to R2 — stub ack
Owner-only status change with email notify
List documents for antrag (owner or ACL)
Multipart upload (25 MB) to R2 + insert metadata
Admin: partial update (camelCase mapped to snake_case columns)
Proxy: any method on a check session
Combined latest CP+VA report from KV
Last N days of CP reports (?days=7)
Same as /health, reachable through api.fund24.io route

worker.summary: /api/check/* wizard is fully unauthenticated yet writes to BAFA_DB.foerdermittel_profile, uploads to R2 and consumes Workers AI on every request — abuse-vulnerable
worker.evidence: check.post('/', ...) — no requireAuth; only KV-session-id (caller-supplied) gates subsequent steps. Profile rows persist with user_id='check-{sessionId}'.

worker.summary: /api/checks/* proxy forwards anything (including body + Authorization header) to a separate Worker without any local auth or rate-limit on top of the global one
worker.evidence: forward() copies request to https://foerdermittel-check-api.froeba-kevin.workers.dev with browser-supplied headers; no token check on the Worker boundary

worker.summary: /api/admin/check-foerdermittel performs outbound HEAD requests to URLs read from foerderprogramme.url with only an 8s timeout — admin-triggered SSRF surface, mass-fan-out (50 per call)
worker.evidence: Promise.all over 50 fetch(p.url, {method: 'HEAD', redirect: 'follow'}) with no host allowlist; redirect=follow lets attackers hop to internal addresses if any malicious url ever lands in the table

worker.summary: /api/reports/preview/:id and /api/reports/download/:token return raw HTML from generated content without explicit XSS sanitization — content is AI-generated
worker.evidence: new Response(generateReportPreview(...), { 'Content-Type': 'text/html' }) — relies on report-html service to escape; no Content-Security-Policy header set on this response

worker.summary: Stripe webhook returns 200 with {received: true, error: '...'} on amount-mismatch and missing-payment cases — Stripe will not retry, log entry only in DB
worker.evidence: lines 100-114 return c.json({received:true, error:...}) instead of 4xx; Stripe treats this as accepted

worker.summary: /api/auth/refresh has no per-IP/per-user rate limit (only the global one); refresh-token rotation is the primary credential-recovery path
worker.evidence: POST /refresh has no loginRateLimit-style guard

worker.summary: /api/me/* uses sub-app .fetch() dispatch which re-enters the foerdermittel/unternehmen sub-app but bypasses the trailing-slash redirect + global pre-mount middleware that already ran on the outer request — works today but is fragile if middleware order changes
worker.evidence: forward() builds a new Request(targetUrl, c.req.raw) and calls foerdermittel.fetch(...) directly

worker.summary: /api/admin/users PATCH allows updating email without re-verifying — admin can silently take over accounts; no audit log shows old vs new email
worker.evidence: allowed list contains 'email'; updateProfile writes directly; audit log just records 'profile update {id}: email'

worker.summary: /api/foerdermittel/cases (POST) auto-creates a foerdermittel_profile from unternehmen with no rate-limit and triggers Workers-AI funnel generation (3000 max_tokens) per program — costly per-call
worker.evidence: ai.run('@cf/meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct-fp8', { max_tokens: 3000 }) on first case creation per program; only the global rate-limit guards

worker.summary: /api/foerdermittel/match runs LLM scoring in batches of 10 with no per-user concurrency cap; can exhaust Workers AI quota if hammered
worker.evidence: for-loop over chunks of 10 with ai.run(...) max_tokens=2000; only guarded by globalRateLimit

worker.summary: /api/admin/news DELETE is hard-delete — no soft-delete or audit log of the deletion
worker.evidence: DELETE FROM news_articles WHERE id = ?; no writeAuditLog call

worker.summary: PATCH /api/admin/provisionen/:id uses dynamic `${k} = ?` SQL, but keys come from zod-parsed object keys (safe). Still, pattern is duplicated across admin/tracker/reports — central helper would reduce drift risk
worker.evidence: for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(d)) sets.push(`${k} = ?`)

worker.summary: Multiple endpoints leak DB-shape error strings in non-production (onError returns err.message) — fine, but verify ENVIRONMENT is set in dev preview deployments to avoid leaking stack info
worker.evidence: error: c.env.ENVIRONMENT === 'production' ? 'Interner Serverfehler' : err.message

worker.summary: /api/verify-payment is unauthenticated — only correlates by sessionId/paymentId. Anyone with a leaked stripe session id could observe payment status (low-impact, no PII beyond reportTitle)
worker.evidence: verifyPayment.post('/verify-payment', async (c) => ...) — no requireAuth

worker.summary: /api/oa/* exposes operational/health metrics publicly (KV-cached CP/VA reports). Likely intentional, but worth confirming nothing-sensitive in those reports
worker.evidence: no requireAuth on any oa.* handler

worker.summary: GAP-001 cert-approve loop registers two routes via shared closure ([approve|reject]) on a single `for ... of` — callable, but Hono line numbers all map to admin.ts:446. Re-check unit tests cover both paths
worker.evidence: for (const [action, next] of [['approve','approved'],['reject','rejected']] as const) { admin.post(`/bafa-cert/:userId/${action}`, ...) }

worker.summary: `scrapeCompanyFromUrl` (called from /api/check) fetches a user-supplied URL. Currently no documented allowlist; SSRF-relevant for an unauthenticated endpoint
worker.evidence: if (body.url) { const scraped = await scrapeCompanyFromUrl(body.url); }

worker.summary: Role-check in /api/admin/users/:id/role only allows 'user'|'admin' — system has 'berater' and 'unternehmen' roles too. Promoting/demoting between those is not exposed (could be intentional)
worker.evidence: if (!['user','admin'].includes(role)) return ...

worker.summary: Several endpoints use `as any` / `as unknown as AiRunner` to cast the Workers-AI binding; safe today but masks future Workers-AI typed breakage (check.ts and inside services)
worker.evidence: type AiRunner = { run(model: string, options: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<any> }; (await (ai as unknown as AiRunner).run(...))

worker.summary: /api/antraege/:id PATCH and other endpoints email owners on phase change with executionCtx.waitUntil — failures swallowed silently; consider failure metric
worker.evidence: c.executionCtx.waitUntil(sendAntragStatusChangedEmail(...))

frontend.summary: getProgramm() calls /api/foerderprogramme/:id but worker exposes /api/foerdermittel/katalog/:id — every /programme/[id] deep link 404s live (confirmed in 01_pages.json: flags BROKEN-all-ids-return-404).
frontend.evidence: return apiCall<Foerderprogramm>(API.FUND24, `/api/foerderprogramme/${id}`)  // should be /api/foerdermittel/katalog/${id} — see app/(public)/programme/[id]/opengraph-image.tsx:15 which uses the correct path

frontend.summary: getProgramme() list also targets /api/foerderprogramme which is not the worker's current listing route (worker uses /api/foerdermittel/katalog, matching sitemap.ts:30 and OG image). High suspicion /programme listing calls a route that does not exist.
app/sitemap.ts:34 reads `data.results` but the katalog worker returns `{success, data, pagination}` — sitemap will always be empty in prod (known issue, still flagged).
frontend.evidence: const data = (await res.json()) as { results?: KatalogItem[]; total?: number }  // actual worker response uses data: […]

frontend.summary: lib/api/check.ts contains massive legacy surface (news, admin, tracker, favoriten, abwicklung, dashboard) pointing at API.CHECK (NEXT_PUBLIC_CHECK_API_URL) that is almost entirely shadowed by fund24.ts on API.FUND24. Candidates for either deletion or accidental use of the wrong worker (e.g. getDashboard, getNachrichten, sendeNachricht still used by pages).
frontend.evidence: getDashboard → apiCall(API.CHECK, `/api/dashboard/${rolle}`, …) called from /dashboard/unternehmen & /dashboard/berater. CHECK base may point to legacy worker / stale URL.

frontend.summary: getNachrichten + sendeNachricht still target API.CHECK at /api/netzwerk/nachrichten — used by /dashboard/berater/nachrichten. If CHECK points to foerdermittel-check-api (precheck) worker this route almost certainly does not exist; nachrichten page may be non-functional.

app/sitemap.ts:52 reads `data.profiles` from /api/netzwerk/berater; the app-side wrapper (check.ts:getBeraterListe) types the response as { berater: BeraterProfil[] }. Shape between sitemap and wrapper is inconsistent — one (or both) is wrong.

createAntrag wrapper normalizes three different response shapes ({data:{caseId}}, {case:{id}}, {id}) — indicates historical API drift that is silently hidden; any change in worker response breaks the fallback.

Many exports in lib/api/* are never imported by any page/component (dead surface): getStats, verifyEmail, deleteDokument, deleteAdminNews, listExpertise, listDienstleistungen, getUnternehmen, createTrackerVorgang, deleteTrackerVorgang, addFavorit (fund24.ts). Reduces clarity and grows attack surface; all still go over production Bearer tokens if invoked.
frontend.evidence: getStats (@6), verifyEmail (auth.ts@103), deleteDokument (@378), deleteAdminNews (@459), listExpertise (berater.ts@123), listDienstleistungen (berater.ts@204), getUnternehmen (unternehmen.ts@57), createTrackerVorgang (@488), deleteTrackerVorgang (@513), addFavorit (@57)

frontend.summary: updateAnfrage wrapper silently coerces any unrecognized status to 'abgelehnt' to keep compatibility with legacy AnfrageStatus union — can result in reject actions happening on user's behalf if UI passes an unexpected status string.

All authenticated fund24.ts helpers resolve the token via useAuth.getState().token at call time and silently pass null on SSR (typeof window === 'undefined' branch). If any of these helpers is ever invoked from a server component/generateMetadata it will issue an unauthenticated request and produce either a 401 or an anonymous response.
frontend.evidence: function token(): string | null { if (typeof window === 'undefined') return null; return useAuth.getState().token }

frontend.summary: updateAdminProvision wrapper widens status to plain string so 'invalid values get a 400 from the worker' — i.e. the UI relies on the worker for enum validation rather than enforcing client-side. Increases likelihood of unguarded state transitions shipping to admin UI.
frontend.evidence: // Widened to string so the UI can pass its own ProvisionStatus type; invalid values get a 400 from the worker.

frontend.summary: Duplicate getTracker/updateTrackerPhase/getNews/getNewsArtikel/getAdminDashboard/getAdminUsers/getAdminProvisionen/getAdminNews/createAdminNews/updateAdminNews/getProvisionVertraege/uploadAbwicklungDokument live in both check.ts and fund24.ts with different auth bases. Name collisions could produce subtle bugs if an import is ever re-pointed.
frontend.evidence: getTracker (check.ts@134 on API.CHECK) vs getTracker (fund24.ts@482 on API.FUND24) — both named the same, exported from different files.

frontend.summary: auth.ts:logout manually clears a document.cookie named 'fund24-auth' but the real HttpOnly cookie set by /api/session is 'fund24-token'. Client-side cookie clear is a no-op on the wrong name (plus HttpOnly cookies cannot be cleared from JS anyway — they require the POST/DELETE to /api/session, which logout doesn't call).

precheck.ts endpoints target API.CHECK (NEXT_PUBLIC_CHECK_API_URL) — worth verifying the precheck worker is still deployed at the same env var that the legacy check endpoints use. If the env var was repurposed, every precheck call fails.

app/antraege/[id]/page.tsx is the only place that mutates Antrag dokumente; per 01_pages.json the route is marked AUTH-LEAK-missing-from-PROTECTED_PREFIXES — an unauthenticated user could still trigger the wrappers (Bearer=null) which will hit the worker anyway and likely return 401s, but no client-side guard blocks this first.
frontend.evidence: Page imports getAntrag/updateAntragStatus/uploadAntragDokument etc. via fund24.ts; Route is flagged AUTH-LEAK in docs/analysis/full-audit/01_pages.json
